name: Master of Fine Arts in Digital Design

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: The 23 courses form a well-architected terminal MFA: shared foundations (Graphic Design/Color, 3D Modelling, Video/Animation) and a two-part history survey feed a common Extended Reality Design Studio (DD 650) that then branches into three parallel two-studio tracks, Animation (651/652), Game Design (653/654), and UI/UX (655/656), before an Independent Project Studio capstone (deepestPrereqChain 3). The gateway-plus-branch structure is elegant and clearly intentional; the only limit is that no plan-of-study groups were parsed, so track rules and elective counts are implicit.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish the plan of study showing the shared core, the DD 650 gateway, and how a student selects and completes one of the three studio tracks plus the capstone.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: The prerequisite graph is sensible and clean: missingCourseCount is 0, the three studio tracks each chain correctly through DD 650 (601/602/603 to 650 to 651 to 652, etc.) to a coherent depth of 3, and the branch point is well-defined. The weakness is that several electives use the vague prose prerequisite 'Basic background in in the use and application of digital media in design' (with a doubled 'in') rather than a course code.
dimensions.suggestion: Replace the repeated prose prerequisite with a concrete gate (e.g., DD 601/DD 602 or portfolio/instructor permission) and fix the 'in in' typo across DD 621/623/625/634.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes or objectives are present, a notable omission for a terminal MFA where a clear statement of creative and professional competencies anchors the degree.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish MFA learning outcomes covering creative research, technical mastery across the three tracks, and portfolio/exhibition readiness.

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: statedTotalCredits is null with no parsed plan, so the total (a terminal MFA is typically ~60 credits) and the balance among 3-credit seminars, 6-credit studios, and history requirements are not transparent; creditReconcileDelta is uncomputable. Given the mix of 3- and 6-credit courses, an explicit tally is important.
dimensions.suggestion: State total credits and show the distribution across foundations, history, the chosen studio track, electives, and the capstone.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: Course descriptions are rich and specific across all 23 courses and titles clearly signal the three-track structure, but the page lacks a plan of study, total credits, and labeled terms, so the track rules must be inferred from prerequisites.
dimensions.suggestion: Add a plan-of-study table and credit summary, and visually group courses by foundation, track, and capstone.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: The curriculum is strongly contemporary: an Extended Reality (XR) gateway studio, dedicated game-design and UI/UX tracks, Physical Computing/Interaction Design, and cross-listed IS courses in User Experience Design and lean-startup Customer Discovery connect creative work to product practice. It maps well to current digital-design employment.
dimensions.suggestion: Name the real-time engines and tools students use (e.g., Unity/Unreal, Figma) and add an explicit AI-assisted content-creation component to stay current.

descriptionQuality.justification: Descriptions are specific and pedagogically clear, e.g., DD 650 framing XR across UI/UX, gaming, and animation, and IS 664 defining customer discovery as evidence-based entrepreneurship; none read as placeholder text.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: Studio-track prerequisites are stated precisely with course codes and no dangling references, but several electives rely on the vague, typo-laden prose prerequisite 'Basic background in in the use and application of digital media in design,' which is ambiguous and inconsistent with the coded studio chain.

missingTopics.rationale: The game and XR studios imply engine work but no course explicitly teaches a real-time engine, the central tool of contemporary game and interactive design.

missingTopics.topic: Technical art, shaders, and procedural content
missingTopics.rationale: Between Environment Design and the game track, there is no explicit technical-art/shader or procedural-generation course, a key employable skill bridging art and engineering.
No course addresses generative-AI tools for image, video, 3D, or animation, now integral to digital-design production pipelines.
For a terminal MFA there is no visible professional-practice, portfolio, or thesis-exhibition course to prepare graduates for the creative job market or academia.

missingTopics.topic: Motion capture and character animation pipeline
missingTopics.rationale: Character/Facial Modeling and Acting for Animators exist, but no course covers the mocap-to-rig-to-animation production pipeline that studios expect.

emergingTrends.rationale: AI image/video/3D generation is transforming design workflows; the foundations and studios should teach critical, hands-on use of these tools.
Game-engine-driven virtual production is reshaping animation and film; the animation and XR studios are positioned to adopt it.
The DD 650 XR gateway should evolve toward current spatial-computing platforms (e.g., Apple Vision Pro, Meta Quest) as they mainstream.
The UI/UX track should foreground accessibility and inclusive-design standards, now a baseline expectation in product design.

emergingTrends.trend: Ethics of AI-generated and immersive media
emergingTrends.rationale: With generative and immersive media raising authorship and consent questions, the history/theory courses could add an ethics strand.

topSuggestions: Publish the plan of study and total credits, showing the shared core, the DD 650 gateway, the three studio tracks, and the capstone.
State MFA program learning outcomes covering creative research, technical mastery, and portfolio/exhibition readiness.
Replace the vague 'Basic background in in the use...' prose prerequisite with concrete course/portfolio gates and fix the 'in in' typo.
Add explicit real-time game-engine and AI-assisted content-creation content to keep the game/XR tracks current.
Add a professional-practice/portfolio (thesis-exhibition) course appropriate to a terminal MFA.
